name: Las Mesas y El Pedernoso — circular desde Mota del Cuervo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mota Del Cuervo offers routes across the open plains of La Mancha, characterized by extensive horizons and generally gentle gradients. The region features the Manjavacas Lagoon Complex, a significant wetland system with saline lagoons ideal for easy cycling. Historic windmills perched on "La Sierra" provide panoramic views and add a cultural element to many routes. This landscape provides varied terrain for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Mota Del Cuervo The most popular touring cycling route is Lagunar de Manjavacas — circular desde Mota del Cuervo, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the natural wetlands of the Manjavacas Lagoon Complex. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mota del Cuervo - Castillo de Belmonte Loop, a moderate 26.7 miles (43.0 km) path. This route features a mix of open plains and offers views towards the historic Belmonte Castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Las Mesas y El Pedernoso — circular desde Mota del Cuervo, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail leading through the agricultural landscapes of the region, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Touring cycling around Mota Del Cuervo is defined by open plains, historic windmills, and significant wetland areas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Mota Del Cuervo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mota Del Cuervo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Mota del Cuervo?
A: The region is characterized by the vast, open plains of La Mancha, offering extensive horizons and generally gentle gradients. While many routes are on paved surfaces, some segments may be unpaved, especially those exploring natural areas like the Manjavacas Lagoon Complex. The flat terrain around the lagoons is particularly suitable for easy cycling.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Mota del Cuervo offers a variety of touring cycling routes catering to different abilities. There are 2 easy routes, 10 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced cyclists.

Q: What natural features can I explore by bike in Mota del Cuervo?
A: A significant natural feature is the &lt;strong&gt;Manjavacas Lagoon Complex Nature Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;, a vital wetland system recognized as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You can explore this area on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086467588/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-desde-mota-del-cuervo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas — circular desde Mota del Cuervo&lt;/a&gt;. The complex is a crucial stopover for migratory birds, offering exceptional birdwatching opportunities, especially around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manjavacas Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Mota del Cuervo is famously home to seven historic &lt;strong&gt;windmills&lt;/strong&gt; perched on 'La Sierra', providing a panoramic 'Balcón de La Mancha' viewpoint over the plains. Some routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359983324/mota-del-cuervo-el-toboso-and-quintanar-de-la-orden-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo, El Toboso, and Quintanar de la Orden Loop&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate these iconic structures. You can also find other points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Quixote and Dulcinea&lt;/a&gt; in nearby El Toboso, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mota del Cuervo?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. These seasons are also excellent for birdwatching at the Manjavacas Lagoon Complex, as migratory birds pass through. Summer can be hot, while winter might be cooler, but cycling is possible year-round depending on personal preference.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mota del Cuervo?
A: Yes, the flat terrain, particularly around the lagoons, makes for excellent family-friendly cycling. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086467588/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-desde-mota-del-cuervo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas — circular desde Mota del Cuervo&lt;/a&gt; are classified as easy and are suitable for families looking for a relaxed ride through nature.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086467588/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-desde-mota-del-cuervo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas — circular desde Mota del Cuervo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359877589/las-mesas-y-el-pedernoso-circular-desde-mota-del-cuervo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Mesas y El Pedernoso — circular desde Mota del Cuervo&lt;/a&gt;, which explore the wetlands and agricultural landscapes respectively.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mota del Cuervo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mota del Cuervo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique combination of historic windmills, the expansive views across the La Mancha plains, and the tranquil, ecologically rich Manjavacas Lagoon Complex.

Q: What is a good moderate-difficulty route that offers a mix of scenery?
A: For a moderate challenge with varied scenery, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359942716/mota-del-cuervo-castillo-de-belmonte-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo - Castillo de Belmonte Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 26.7-mile (43.0 km) route combines open plains with views towards the historic Belmonte Castle, offering a diverse cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through other towns or villages?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359983324/mota-del-cuervo-el-toboso-and-quintanar-de-la-orden-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo, El Toboso, and Quintanar de la Orden Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you through the charming town of El Toboso, known as the home of Don Quixote's Dulcinea, and Quintanar de la Orden, allowing you to experience more of the regional culture and architecture.

Q: How long are the touring cycling routes in Mota del Cuervo?
A: The routes vary in length to suit different preferences. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31442481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manjavacas Lagoon – Manjavacas Lagoon Complex loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 5.3 miles (8.5 km), while longer moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359983324/mota-del-cuervo-el-toboso-and-quintanar-de-la-orden-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo, El Toboso, and Quintanar de la Orden Loop&lt;/a&gt; extend to over 41.6 miles (67.0 km).
tips: In the place called “La Sierra”, there are the seven windmills of Mota del Cuervo. Outstanding set among those existing in the region and that in 1967 acquired the geotouristic name of "Balcón de La Mancha" due to the vast and extensive horizon that can be seen from them.

The sanctuary is a construction made of masonry with a hipped tile roof. Inside, it has a rectangular plan with a flat head. The nave is divided into four sections by barely hinted transverse arches that rest on Tuscan ilasters, with a barrel vault and lunettes.
Nice and quiet place especially at sunset where the landscape offers very picturesque views.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Manjavacas
